personality_one_liner: An immersive, cinematic, and dark-themed reporting tool where community problems are elevated through full-screen imagery and clean glass overlays.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room for cinematic feel; glass surfaces require larger padding to separate from underlying imagery.

do's: Use glass for the bottom tab bar, floating right-side interaction icons on the feed, and bottom sheet handles.
Apply vertical gradient scrim (transparent to 80% surfaceInverse) at the bottom 40% of the full-screen reel to guarantee text readability.
Pair blur with translucent tinted base layered underneath (≥75% of surface in dark mode).
dont's: Never blur body text regions, form fields, or entire screens.
Do not use glass effects on Android low-end fallback; use solid surfaceSecondary instead.

screens.name: HomeFeed
screens.purpose: A vertical, full-screen immersive scrolling feed for showcasing civic problems.

states.loading: Centered brand-tinted spinner on deep dark surface.
states.empty: Stylized camera illustration indicating no reports yet.
states.error: Readable text with a retry button over a subtle glass card.

screens.layout: Full-screen vertical FlatList (paging enabled). Video/Image occupies 100% height and width. Bottom 40% has a dark gradient scrim (transparent -> #050505 at 80% opacity) layered below the text (Caption, Category, Profile). Right side holds a vertical stack of glass-backed icon buttons (Like, Comment, Share). Tab bar sits at the bottom using glass effect.

screens.name: Search
screens.purpose: Find specific problems by category or discover users.

states.loading: Skeleton rows reflecting either user profiles or feed thumbnails.
states.empty: No results found illustration with refined empty text.

screens.layout: Top sticky search bar with glass effect. Immediately below, a horizontal scrollable segmented control (Users | Problems). Below that, a masonry or 2-column grid for problem images. When 'Problems' is active, show category chips (Electricity, Wastage, Road, etc.) horizontally below the search bar to filter results.

screens.name: CreatePost
screens.purpose: Capture a problem and describe it before posting.

states.error: Failed to post alert or retry snackbar.

screens.layout: Step 1: Full-screen camera view or gallery grid. Step 2: Form screen with the selected image as a small thumbnail at the top. Below, single-column form: Description (multiline), Category selector (chips), Tag users (input). Submit button sticky at the bottom.

screens.name: Profile
screens.purpose: Showcase user's reported civic issues and personal details.

states.loading: Skeleton layout for cover, avatar, and grid.

screens.layout: Top half: Cover banner (real photo or brand gradient) with an overlapping avatar. Name, Username, Location, and Slogan in a centered, clean typography layout. Bottom half: 3-column grid of reported problems (no gaps or 1px divider). Sticky header with glass effect when scrolling down.

feed_pothole_problem.alt: Urban street puddle reflecting orange building with broken road.

feed_wastage_problem.alt: Trolley with trash in dirty alley with pile of rubbish.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the '6 Glass / Luxe DARK' personality. The app should feel immersive and cinematic.

Implement the full-screen vertical swipe feed mimicking Reels: The image must fill the safe area. Add a strict vertical gradient scrim (transparent to 80% surfaceInverse) at the bottom to ensure text readability.
The right-side interaction icons (Like, Comment, Share) must be stacked vertically and placed on glass-effect circular containers.
Navigation bottom tab bar MUST use a frosted glass effect with translucency, allowing the full-screen feed images to blur underneath.
For CreatePost, ensure the camera/gallery interaction is smooth and the form layout is single-column with a sticky submit button.
Images for feed posts MUST NOT have generic placeholders. Use the provided real-world examples of civic issues.
Ensure 'swiping up' functionality on the feed works flawlessly, ideally using a FlatList with `pagingEnabled={true}`.
No blue or purple colors are permitted anywhere. Stick strictly to the emerald/dark palette.
Ensure the Google Social Login button on the auth screen follows the brand design, avoiding generic default web-like buttons.
